notes.note: A letter of recommendation from Cicero to Q. Cornificius, proconsul of Africa. The S. is salutem (dicit), "sends greetings"; the broken spacing of CORNIFICIO is a quirk of the manuscript transmission.

notes.anchor: made heirs of his own kind
notes.note: Under Roman law a will named heredes who took the whole estate (and its debts) jointly; Turius left it to a circle of fellow-businessmen rather than to family, hence "of his own kind."

A commendatio was a formal letter of introduction asking a magistrate to favour the bearer; the whole book-13 genre. Here Cicero notes the men have already been so well treated that thanks, not a fresh request, are owed.

notes.anchor: Eros Turius, the freedman of Q. Turius
notes.note: A freedman bore his patron's nomen (here Turius); freedmen often managed a patron's business and could attempt to divert an inheritance, as Eros is alleged to be doing.
